2606.12412.title: Reroute, Don't Remove: Recoverable Visual Token Routing for Vision-Language Models
2606.12412.paper_summary: Vision-language models (VLMs) project images into hundreds to thousands of visual tokens, making decoder inference expensive in both attention computation and KV-cache memory. Existing visual-token reduction methods largely follow a rank-and-remove paradigm: they score visual tokens, keep a compact subset, and permanently discard the rest. We show that this irreversible action is fragile because visual-token importance changes across decoder depth; tokens ranked low at one stage may become relevant in later layers, especially for grounding-sensitive queries. We propose Reroute, a training-free plug-in that replaces removal with recoverable routing. At each routing stage, selected vision tokens pass through decoder blocks, while deferred tokens bypass the stage and re-enter the candidate pool at the next routing decision. Reroute reuses existing attention-score ranking rules and stage-wise schedules, preserving the theoretical TFLOPs and KV-cache budget class of the pruning method it augments. Across FastV, PDrop, and Nüwa variants on LLaVA-1.5 and Qwen backbones, reroute improves grounding under aggressive token reduction while maintaining general VQA performance. These results suggest that VLM token reduction should not be viewed only as irreversible pruning, but also as recoverable routing. The code can be found here: https://github.com/elmma/mllm-reroute/
2606.12412.paper_summary_zh: 視覺語言模型（VLMs）將圖像投影到數百到數千個視覺標記中，使得解碼器推理在注意力計算和KV快取記憶體方面變得昂貴。現有的視覺標記減少方法主要遵循一種排名和移除的範式：它們對視覺標記進行評分，保留一個緊湊的子集，並永久丟棄其餘部分。我們表明，這種不可逆的行為是脆弱的，因為視覺標記的重要性在解碼器深度中會發生變化；在某一階段排名較低的標記在後面的層中可能變得相關，特別是對於對基礎敏感的查詢。我們提出了Reroute，一個無需訓練的插件，將移除替換為可恢復的路由。在每個路由階段，選定的視覺標記通過解碼器塊，而延遲的標記則跳過該階段，並在下一個路由決策中重新進入候選池。Reroute重用現有的注意力分數排名規則和階段性計劃，保留其增強的修剪方法的理論TFLOPs和KV快取預算類別。在LLaVA-1.5和Qwen主幹上的FastV、PDrop和Nüwa變體中，Reroute在激進的標記減少下改善了基礎，同時保持了通用的VQA性能。這些結果表明，VLM標記減少不應僅被視為不可逆的修剪，還應視為可恢復的路由。代碼可以在這裡找到：https://github.com/elmma/mllm-reroute/
2606.12412.author: Cheng-Yu Yang et.al.
2606.12412.authors: Cheng-Yu Yang, Shao-Yuan Lo, Yu-Lun Liu
